title: Manager (association football)

infobox.value: is the person who has overall responsibility for the running of a [football team](/wiki/Football_team "Football team"). They have wide-ranging responsibilities, including selecting the team, choosing the tactics, recruiting and [transferring](/wiki/Transfer_(association_football) "Transfer (association football)") players, negotiating player contracts, and speaking to the media. In professional football, a manager is usually appointed by and answerable to the club's board of directors, but at an amateur level the manager may have total responsibility for the running of a club.

Saffer, Paul (21 May 2016). ["The unsackables: Europe's longest-serving coaches"](https://www.uefa.com/news/0253-0d8055f8b528-566b322fe89a-1000--the-unsackables-europe-s-longest-serving-coaches/). UEFA. Retrieved 7 November 2020.

["Europe's top coaches convene in Nyon"](https://www.uefa.com/news-media/news/0225-0e9191e92e37-2d7ab5a03f80-1000--europe-s-top-coaches-convene-in-nyon/). UEFA. 2 September 2015. Retrieved 7 November 2020.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Head coach of an association football team

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f2/Alex_Ferguson_02.jpg/250px-Alex_Ferguson_02.jpg)](/wiki/File:Alex_Ferguson_02.jpg)

[Alex Ferguson](/wiki/Alex_Ferguson "Alex Ferguson") is the winner of the most English [Manager of the Year](/wiki/Premier_League_Manager_of_the_Year "Premier League Manager of the Year") awards, all won during his tenure as manager of [Manchester United](/wiki/Manchester_United "Manchester United"). He is the UEFA coaching ambassador.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e9/George_Ramsay.jpg/250px-George_Ramsay.jpg)](/wiki/File:George_Ramsay.jpg)

[George Ramsay](/wiki/George_Ramsay_(footballer,_born_1855) "George Ramsay (footballer, born 1855)") has been described as the world's first football manager. He managed Aston Villa from 1886 to 1926, during which time he established Villa as the most successful club in England.

In [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football"), the **manager** is the person who has overall responsibility for the running of a [football team](/wiki/Football_team "Football team"). They have wide-ranging responsibilities, including selecting the team, choosing the tactics, recruiting and [transferring](/wiki/Transfer_(association_football) "Transfer (association football)") players, negotiating player contracts, and speaking to the media. In professional football, a manager is usually appointed by and answerable to the club's board of directors, but at an amateur level the manager may have total responsibility for the running of a club.

## Responsibilities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Manager_(association_football)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Responsibilities")]

See also: [UEFA coaching licences](/wiki/UEFA_coaching_licences "UEFA coaching licences") and [AFC Professional Coaching Diploma](/wiki/AFC_Professional_Coaching_Diploma "AFC Professional Coaching Diploma")

The manager's responsibilities in a professional football club usually include (but are not limited to) the following:[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

* Selecting the team of players for matches, and their [formation](/wiki/Formation_(association_football) "Formation (association football)").
* Planning the strategy, and instructing the players on the [pitch](/wiki/Association_football_pitch "Association football pitch").
* Motivating players before and during a match.
* Delegating duties to the first team coach and the [coaching and medical staff](/wiki/Coaching_staff "Coaching staff").
* Scouting for young but talented players for eventual [training](/wiki/Training_ground_(association_football) "Training ground (association football)") in the [youth academy](/wiki/Youth_system "Youth system") or the [reserves](/wiki/Reserve_team "Reserve team"), and encouraging their development and improvement.
* Buying and selling players in the [transfer market](/wiki/Transfer_(association_football) "Transfer (association football)"), including [loans](/wiki/Loan_(association_football) "Loan (association football)").
* Facing the media in pre-match and post-match interviews.

Some of the above responsibilities may be shared with a [director of football](/wiki/Director_of_football "Director of football") or [sporting director](/wiki/Sporting_director "Sporting director"), and are at times delegated to an assistant manager or club coach.

Additionally, depending on the club, some minor[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] responsibilities include:

* Marketing the club, most especially for ticket admission, sponsorship and merchandising.
* Growing turnover and keeping the club profitable.

These responsibilities are more common among managers of small clubs.

* [Football Manager](/wiki/Football_Manager "Football Manager"), a simulation video game for playing as a manager.
